bugs.error_message: P0001: Use this spell action to cast instant and sorcery cards
bugs.root_cause: CardActionSheet always called castCardFromHand for all hand cards. Counterspells require putCounterSpellOnStack with a targetStackItemId.
bugs.fix: castSpell action now checks doesCardRequireStackTarget. If true and stack has items, routes to putCounterSpellOnStack targeting the top stack item.

bugs.error_message: V4 controller: combat damage never resolved — attacking dealt no damage
bugs.root_cause: V4 removed all manual step controls and left only PASS. advance_step from combat_damage to end_of_combat does not call resolve_combat_damage, and V4 never invoked the RPC, so passing priority through combat_damage skipped damage entirely.
bugs.fix: Added combat damage resolution to PriorityPanel: during combat_damage step the active player sees a Resolve button that calls resolve_combat_damage. Tracks returned damage_stage (first_strike vs regular) to handle the two-pass first/double strike flow; switches back to PASS once the regular pass resolves. Resets stage on leaving the step.

bugs.root_cause: Condition was: if (step === 'declare_blockers' && incomingAttackers.l)
bugs.fix: Changed to: if (step === 'declare_blockers' && incomingAttackers.l)

bugs.error_message: V4: after declaring blockers the game is stuck — DeclareBlockersLayout stays shown and step never advances
bugs.root_cause: layoutState returned declare_blockers whenever step===declare_blockers AND incomingAttackers>0, without checking priority. After the defender confirmed and passed priority, step was still declare_blockers with attackers present, so the layout kept showing and trapped them — neither player could complete the priority pass to advance. The declare_attackers layout already gated on hasPriority; declare_blockers did not.
bugs.fix: Added && hasPriority to the declare_blockers layoutState condition. After the defender passes, priority leaves them, the layout exits to the board (Wait), and the active player can pass to advance to combat_damage.

bugs.root_cause: scale: 0.85, opacity: 0 → 0.9, opacity: 0
bugs.fix: Changed scale: 0.85, opacity: 0 → 0.9, opacity: 0

bugs.root_cause: Centering used Tailwind -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2, but the panel is a Framer motion.div with animate={{scale}}, which writes an inline transform that overrides the Tailwind translate utilities. The centering translate was lost so the top-left corner sat at screen center.
bugs.fix: Center via flexbox on the backdrop (flex items-center justify-center) and make the panel a child of it. The panel keeps its scale/opacity animation without needing transform-based positioning.
